titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the National Humanities Institute, Yale University, Records
National Humanities Institute, Yale University, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 17, 2019 at 07:12

did.unittitle: National Humanities Institute, Yale University, records
did.abstract: The records consist of program proposals, correspondence, memoranda, film, annual reports, financial statements, and papers documenting the National Humanities Institute at Yale University.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 37 (YRG 37), Records of associations, institutions, centers, and organizations affiliated with Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the records is restricted. See Collection Contents for details.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: National Humanities Institute, Yale University, Records (RU 559). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by subject and record type.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of program proposals, correspondence, memoranda, film, annual reports, financial statements, and papers documenting the National Humanities Institute at Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

did.unittitle: Syllabi, exams, bibliographies for courses of K. Sklar, UCLA
Readings in the Social History of American Women,'' compiled by K. Sklar, UCLA
Nominated Fellows, 1975-1976: preliminary correspondence respondence (by institution)
Invited: Knoll, Robert E. (U. of Nebraska)
Invited: Woodard, Calvin (U. of Va. Law School)
Special Programs: Council for Religion in Independent Schools
Special Programs: The National Humanities Faculty
Visiting Fellows, Final Papers, and General Files
Peter Berger: "Mediating Structures and National Policy: Family, Church, Neighborhood and Voluntary Associations"
Anthony Garvan: "American Studies and the New Humanities"
Robert Heilbroner: "The Decline of Business Civilization"
N. Scott Momaday: "In Wonder and Delight: Indian Oral Tradition"
E. P. Thompson: "The Sale of Wives in England in the 18th and 19th Centuries"
Erich Heller: "Psychoanalysis and the Misinterpretation of Literature"
James Ackerman: "Judgments of Value: Reflections on Art and History"
